retrieved_docs.title: Facial Implants -- Complications
retrieved_docs.content: The risk of bony erosion occurs more frequently with solid implants than with porous implants. The erosion depth increases as the implant size increases or becomes more rigid. [35] Theories as to why bony erosion occurs include mobility, inflammation, and stress on the overlying soft tissue.

retrieved_docs.title: Facial Implants -- Enhancing Healthcare Team Outcomes
retrieved_docs.content: The Food and Drug Administration closely monitors implantable medical devices, but the range of FDA-approved materials is ever-growing. The team must inform the patient of the risks of using the selected implantable device. For instance, silicone materials show no increase in reactive oxygen species or genotoxicity but may cause bony erosion when placed on the chin, as previously mentioned. [37]

retrieved_docs.title: Facial Implants -- Complications
retrieved_docs.content: Improper implant size, shape, or placement can result in lip incompetence. For chin implants, active engagement of the mentalis muscle, which strains to compensate for weakness, increases pressure on the underlying implant. Placing the implant at the inferior mandibular border where the cortex is thicker and further from the mentalis insertion has been shown to cause less resorption. Implant fixation helps eliminate micro-movements that can contribute to continual erosion. Although controversial, erosion has been reported to be self-limiting. Fixation is assumed to play a role in self-limiting erosion.

retrieved_docs.title: Dental Implants -- Complications
retrieved_docs.content: The most common postoperative complication is incision line opening. The design of the removable interim prosthesis is involved, it is corrected. The patient is instructed to rinse 2-3 times daily with chlorhexidine. If granulation process extends for more than two weeks, epithelial margin trimming can be done. If implants become exposed during the healing period, no attempt should be made to cover them with tissue. Rather denture is relieved aggressively over the area with implant exposure. The mobility of the implant during healing is unusual but may occur, mostly accompanied by a radiolucent zone around the implant. Whatever may be the cause, the implant should be removed. Signs and symptoms of failure for an implant are horizontal mobility  greater than 0.5 mm, rapid progressive bone loss, pain during percussion, uncontrolled exudate, generalized radiolucency around the implant, more than one half of the bone is lost around the implant and last the implants inserted in poor position, making them useless for prosthetic support. A success rate of 85%at the end of 5 year period and 80% at the end of 10 year period are minimum criteria for success. [15] [16] [17] [18]

retrieved_docs.content: While obtaining orthodontic anchorage with the bracket like screw head, turning the ligature around the screw will make it impossible to keep the area free of inflammation. The ligature should be placed in the slot perpendicular to the wire on top of the screw [10]

retrieved_docs.content: Infection can occur if the transmucosal portion around the screw is not entirely smooth [10]

retrieved_docs.title: Dental Implants -- Complications
retrieved_docs.content: Various complications and problems can be encountered during surgery and postoperatively. Perforated buccal or lingual plates can be seen during the procedure. In case of an elliptical /eccentric preparation, a wider implant can be used if possible. If not, pack the osteotomy with autogenous graft, compress it, and place implant again. Bleeding in the floor of the mouth can occur from the lingual artery or facial artery injury. So absolute care has to be taken during osteotomy preparation.  Nerve injury can lead to altered nerve sensation in the form of anesthesia, paresthesia or hyperesthesia. Consequently, the surgical landmark is often set conservatively 2mm above the mandibular canal.

retrieved_docs.title: Lip Implants -- Complications
retrieved_docs.content: Extrusion (less than 1%): extrusion of an implant may occur if severe asymmetry and wound breakdown occur. This extrusion is avoidable by using the correct size implant, maintaining proper dissection planes, maintaining a small/narrow submucosal pocket, and using sound suture technique to close the commissure incisions. Instructing the patient to avoid heavy manipulation of the implanted area (e.g., talking, chewing, massaging) for the first several days after implantation may also help prevent malposition/extrusion.

retrieved_docs.content: Primary stability is poor where the cortex is thinner than 0.5 mm and density of trabecular bone is low [10]

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 32 -- Level 3 -- Community 2025
retrieved_docs.content: Cervical cerclage suture present, polyp of cervix affecting pregnancy, previous operation to cervix affecting pregnancy, and rigid cervix uteri affecting pregnancy are all findings that have the cervix uteri as their anatomic site. These entities represent structural or pathological conditions of the cervix that can influence pregnancy outcomes. A cervical cerclage suture is placed to reinforce a weakened cervix and prevent premature dilation, while a cervical polyp can interfere with implantation or cause bleeding during gestation. Prior cervical surgery may alter cervical integrity and increase the risk of cervical insufficiency, and a rigid cervix can impede normal cervical remodeling required for labor. Recognizing that each of these findings is located in the cervix uteri helps clinicians assess cervical health, anticipate potential obstetric complications, and tailor management strategies to support a safe pregnancy.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 26 -- Level 4 -- Community 2885
retrieved_docs.content: COMPLICATIONS MAINLY RELATED TO PREGNANCY is classified as a subcategory within the broader group of Complications of pregnancy, childbirth and the puerperium, indicating that these health issues are primarily associated with the gestational period and its immediate aftermath.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 23 -- Level 3 -- Community 2535
retrieved_docs.content: Female infertility (العقم لدى الإناث) and endometrial implantation (انتباذ بطاني رحمي) are interconnected medical conditions. Prevention and control measures are applicable to both female infertility and endometrial implantation, and the two conditions are related, indicating that endometrial implantation can contribute to or be associated with female infertility and that managing endometrial pathology is important in the care of women with infertility.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 33 -- Level 1 -- Community 109
retrieved_docs.content: disorders, and obstetric interventions together form a comprehensive framework for risk assessment, care planning, and management of maternal‑fetal outcomes during pregnancy, delivery, and the postpartum period.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 15 -- Level 3 -- Community 1692
retrieved_docs.content: Cerclage (procedure) is clinically associated with cervical insufficiency (قصور عنق الرحم), indicating that this surgical technique is used to support a weakened cervix and prevent premature dilation in patients with this condition. In addition, optional termination of pregnancy (إنهاء الحمل - اختياري) is also clinically associated with cervical insufficiency, reflecting that pregnancy termination may be considered as a management option in severe cases where the insufficiency poses significant risk to maternal or fetal health. Both interventions are relevant clinical considerations in the assessment and treatment of cervical insufficiency.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 25 -- Level 4 -- Community 2811
retrieved_docs.content: Other complications of pregnancy are clinically associated with إنهاء الحمل – اختياري (optional termination of pregnancy) and also have a relationship with اضطراب رحمي (uterine disorder). This indicates that uterine disorders are considered among the other complications of pregnancy and that optional termination of pregnancy can be clinically linked to these pregnancy‑related complications.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 2071
retrieved_docs.content: Pregnancy-Related Procedures comprise a range of interventions used to manage complex obstetric conditions and facilitate safe delivery. Among these, the surgical treatment of ectopic pregnancy and abdominal pregnancy represents critical operative approaches for addressing abnormal implantation of the embryo, thereby preventing life‑threatening complications for the mother. In addition, Vaginal Assisted Delivery is included as a procedural option that supports the progression of labor and assists in the birth of the infant when additional support is required. Together, these child procedures under the umbrella of Pregnancy-Related Procedures illustrate the spectrum of surgical and assisted techniques employed in modern obstetric care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 1447
retrieved_docs.content: Cerclage (procedure) and REPRODUCTIVE HISTORY POOR are both clinically associated with قصور عنق الرحم. قصور عنق الرحم, also known as cervical insufficiency, is a condition in which the cervix is unable to maintain its structural integrity during pregnancy, increasing the risk of premature dilation and preterm delivery. The cerclage procedure involves placing a suturing stitch around the cervix to reinforce it and prevent premature opening, thereby directly addressing the mechanical weakness characteristic of قصور عنق الرحم. Additionally, a poor reproductive history—such as prior miscarriages, preterm births, or other adverse pregnancy outcomes—has been linked to a higher likelihood of developing قصور عنق الرحم, indicating that past obstetric complications can serve as a clinical indicator for this cervical disorder. Recognizing both the therapeutic role of cerclage and the predictive value of reproductive history is essential for early identification and management of patients at risk for قصور عنق الرحم.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 22 -- Level 3 -- Community 1460
retrieved_docs.content: Infertility bij vrouw van cervicale oorsprorg is classified as a type of اضطراب في عنق الرحم (cervical disorder) and is a specific subtype of العقم لدى الإناث (female infertility). The pathophysiology of this cervical disorder, of العقم لدى الإناث, and of انتباذ بطاني رحمي (endometrial implantation) are interrelated, indicating shared underlying mechanisms that can influence reproductive outcomes. The entity “Allowed qualifier” is used to denote these pathophysiological connections and also links gout and other crystal arthropathies to the same pathophysiological framework. Recognizing how cervical‑origin infertility, broader female infertility, endometrial implantation, and systemic crystal arthropathies are connected helps clinicians assess underlying causes and tailor comprehensive management strategies for affected patients.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2431
retrieved_docs.content: Cervical shortening in pregnancy is categorized as a component of other complications of birth and puerperium that influence the management of the mother, and it specifically encompasses the sub‑condition of cervical shortening occurring in the second trimester.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 11 -- Level 3 -- Community 1532
retrieved_docs.content: Other complications of pregnancy are uterine conditions that arise within the uterus and are clinically associated with optional termination of pregnancy (إنهاء الحمل - اختياري). This relationship underscores that these complications are localized to the uterine environment and may influence or be influenced by decisions regarding elective pregnancy termination, highlighting the importance of uterine health in the management of pregnancy‑related problems.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 20 -- Level 2 -- Community 724
retrieved_docs.content: its relevance for device regulation and clinical monitoring in gynecologic care. Vaginal bleeding (نزيف مهبلي) is identified as a clinical manifestation that is associated with high‑risk patient status, highlighting the need for careful evaluation and management in patients presenting with this symptom. Together, these entities illustrate how various gynecologic and obstetric conditions are organized within standardized terminologies, linked to common risk factors such as high‑risk status and poor reproductive history, and connected to specific clinical interventions or classifications that guide patient assessment and treatment.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 1895
retrieved_docs.content: Removal of cerclage from cervix is a medical intervention that is directly related to the cervical cerclage procedure (إجراء تطويق عنق الرحم). This step involves extracting the sutures that were placed around the cervix to reinforce cervical competence and prevent premature dilation, and it represents a specific phase within the overall management of cervical insufficiency. Recognizing the connection between the removal of the cerclage and the broader cerclage procedure is essential for appropriate patient care and timing of intervention.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 34 -- Level 4 -- Community 2814
retrieved_docs.content: Achtergebleven IUD tijdens zwangerschap, which refers to a retained intrauterine device during pregnancy, is classified as an “Other specified complication of pregnancy” in both the antepartum and postpartum contexts. This dual categorization indicates that the presence of a retained IUD can constitute a distinct obstetric complication before delivery (antepartum) as well as after delivery (postpartum), underscoring its importance for monitoring and management throughout the entire perinatal period.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 15 -- Level 3 -- Community 1719
retrieved_docs.content: Infertility in women of cervical origin is classified as a specific type of cervical disorder (اضطراب في عنق الرحم). This condition is a subset of the broader category of female infertility (العقم لدى الإناث). Female infertility is closely related to endometrial implantation abnormalities (انتباذ بطاني رحمي), indicating that disturbances in the uterine lining can contribute to the inability to conceive. Radiotherapy (radioterapie) is recognized as an approved therapeutic option for cervical disorders and for endometrial implantation abnormalities; in certain clinical scenarios where these pathologies underlie female infertility, radiotherapy may be considered as part of the management strategy. Understanding the hierarchical relationship between cervical‑origin infertility, general female infertility, and endometrial implantation, as well as the role of radiotherapy, helps clinicians tailor comprehensive treatment plans for patients experiencing infertility.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 1 -- Community 209
retrieved_docs.content: constitute major complications of pregnancy, childbirth, and the puerperium. Recognizing the placement of each specific condition within its broader category supports comprehensive assessment, risk stratification, and coordinated management of pregnant patients.
